title: DBMS and Uniface Characteristics
text: DBMS and Uniface Characteristics Before developing database connectors for Uniface, you need to be familiar with the DBMS and Uniface characteristics that are relevant for the developer of a DBMS connector. Types of DBMS DBMS connectors for these two types of DBMS differ considerably. You still need to implement all the same connector requests (even if implementing them means that no action is performed at run time) and use more or less the same service functions, because this is the only way of supporting the full functionality of the Uniface DBMS connector interface. Related Links Databases Uniface recognizes two kinds of database management systems—those that can manage individual fields within records (field-level DBMS) and those that manage records or files (record-level DBMS).
